observatory_hook: Authority-weighted evidence matrix | regulator-action timeline | jurisdiction map.
interpretation_note: This family remains the strongest direct official-action layer already supporting bounded shared claims, but its geography is still narrower than the atlas-wide ambition.

observatory_hook: Harm-taxonomy note | conceptual-support panel | related-work table | Route-A appendix-only methods note.
interpretation_note: These families remain useful for methods, comparator baselines, and taxonomy framing, but not as stand-alone authority for legal status or allegation truth.

observatory_hook: Jurisdiction/language map | lead-to-authority upgrade funnel | rare-language methods note.
interpretation_note: This family keeps the language-aware discovery surface visible and upgradeable without overstating machine-translation outputs as final legal or normative conclusions.

observatory_hook: Lead queue | corroboration backlog | source-upgrade funnel. | Residual-category queue
interpretation_note: These families remain governance-first frontier queues: useful for novelty and residual-category stress tests, but still below seeded evidence and shared-claim support.

observatory_hook: Research-integrity dashboard facet | publisher-notice upgrade funnel | scholarly-integrity timeline | Route-A limitations panel.
interpretation_note: This family remains publication-useful because it separates direct notice text, archived notice text, mediated metadata, and blocked/gated rows instead of flattening them.

observatory_hook: Regulator-to-court escalation matrix | procedural-posture timeline | Route-B blocker panel. | Jurisdiction/language map
interpretation_note: These remain the decisive thin families for stronger Route-B claims: sf08 still gates court-text depth, sf09 still gates buyer-side or public-companion procurement depth, and sf11 still gates incident-specific peer-reviewed security depth.

observatory_hook: Security/agentic crosswalk | technique-coverage heatmap | case-context-versus-cautious-context panel. | Security-edge-case bridge
interpretation_note: These families remain valuable for mechanism, remediation, and interoperability context. The sf10 issuer-notice layer is now stronger because Langflow has formal advisory depth and the CrewAI pair has exact-fix visibility, but the structured-security cluster still should not be narrated as final authority on realized incident truth.
